name: Classical Syriac

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Classical Syriac (clas1252).

notes: Skeleton. Classical Syriac, a Semitic (Afroasiatic, Central Semitic/Aramaic) language written in the Syriac abjad. The 22 consonant letters are mapped to their plosive (non-spirantized) IPA values; begadkepat spirantization of ⟨ܒ ܓ ܕ ܟ ܦ ܬ⟩ is phonologically conditioned and not encoded here. Vowels are written only with optional pointing (or as matres lectionis ⟨ܐ ܘ ܝ⟩); unpointed text leaves most vowels unrecovered, so a high PER floor is expected. Gemination and stress are also unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Classical Syriac (clas1252)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
